content.problemStatement:
You are given an array A=[A1,A2,‚Ä¶,AN]A = [A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N]A=[A1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã] containing NNN elements. You are also given QQQ **ordered** pairs of integers, where the iii-th pair is denoted (xi,yi)(x_i, y_i)(xi‚Äã,yi‚Äã).

A subsequence of AAA, say [Ai1,Ai2,‚Ä¶,Aik][A_{i_1}, A_{i_2}, \ldots, A_{i_k}][Ai1‚Äã‚Äã,Ai2‚Äã‚Äã,‚Ä¶,Aik‚Äã‚Äã], is said to be magical if the following condition is satisfied:


For each 1‚â§j<k1 \leq j \lt k1‚â§j<k, (Aij,Aij+1)(A_{i_j}, A_{i_{j+1}})(Aij‚Äã‚Äã,Aij+1‚Äã‚Äã) must be among the given QQQ pairs. That is, each consecutive pair of elements in this subsequence must be among the given QQQ pairs.
Find the length of the longest magical subsequence of AAA, and print the **indices** of one such longest subsequence.

If there are multiple longest magical subsequences, you may print the indices of **any** of them.

**Note:** Array contains at least one valid subsequence of length greater than or equal to 222.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN, denoting the length of the array.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers representing the elements of the array.
The third line of each test case contains an integer QQQ, denoting the number of pairs.
The iii-th of the next QQQ lines contains two space-separated integers xix_ixi‚Äã and yiy_iyi‚Äã ‚Äî the iii-th **ordered pair**.


Output Format
For each test case, print two lines.



The first line should contain a single integer KKK ‚Äî the length of the longest magical subsequence of AAA.
The second line should contain KKK space-separated integers in ascending order, denoting the **indices** of the magical subsequence you found.

In case there are multiple answers, print **any** of them.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.
The sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:  The length of the longest magical subsequence is 333. One such subsequence is [3,8,6][3, 8, 6][3,8,6], given by indices [2,4,7][2, 4, 7][2,4,7]. The adjacent pairs are (3,8)(3, 8)(3,8) and (8,6)(8, 6)(8,6), both of which are among the given pairs.
Test case 222: The length of the longest magical subsequence is 555 ‚Äî take the entire array. Adjacent elements are (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) and (2,1)(2, 1)(2,1), both of which are in the given pairs.
Test case 333: The length of the longest magical subsequence is 333. One such subsequence of length 333 is [1,1,4][1, 1, 4][1,1,4], with indices [1,3,4][1, 3, 4][1,3,4]. Adjacent elements are (1,1)(1, 1)(1,1) and (1,4)(1, 4)(1,4), which are among the given pairs.
